content.problemStatement:
Chef is making cookies for NNN kids. He wants to give everyone an **equal** number of cookies. He must give at least 111 cookie to each kid.

Chef already has MMM cookies.

He can destroy or make 111 cookie in 111 second.

Find the **minimum** time after which he can evenly distribute the cookies between all kids.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input.

The first and only line of each test case contains NNN and MMM - the number of kids and the initial number of cookies.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum time such that Chef can evenly distribute all his cookies.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and M — the number of kids and the initial number of cookies.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of seconds required so that Chef can have a total number of cookies that is divisible by N and at least N (so each kid receives at least one cookie).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: nearest valid multiple of 5 is 15 (distance 2).
Test case 2: nearest valid multiple of 5 is 20 (distance 2).
Test case 3: N=4, the smallest allowed multiple is 4, distance |1-4|=3.
Test case 4: 18 is already a multiple of 2, distance 0.
For (3,7) the nearest multiples are 6 (distance 1) and 9 (distance 2) → answer 1.\nFor (10,5) the only allowed multiple is 10 (distance 5).

solution.approach:
For a given N and M we need the closest integer multiple of N that is >= N. Let q = M / N (integer division). The two candidate multiples are:
- lower = q * N (if lower >= N, otherwise it is invalid)
- upper = (q + (M % N != 0 ? 1 : 0)) * N (always >= N)
The answer is the minimum of |M - lower| (when lower is valid) and |M - upper|. If M itself is a multiple of N and M >= N, the answer is 0. This runs in O(1) per test case.
Edge cases handled: when M < N (lower is invalid) and when M is already a valid multiple.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
